programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Outside In: Lost in Academia
programme.desc: Japanese culture is in the global spotlight like never before. Dive into the work of international scholars exploring Japan in bold, original ways, uncovering unique perspectives and deep insights.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Today's Close-Up
programme.desc: Japanese authorities are trying to recover thousands of toy guns that can fire live rounds with potentially lethal effect. NHK investigated how these dangerous products entered circulation.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia, and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.desc: Specialists rebuild a sturdy, mud-walled storehouse to safeguard cultural assets, scientifically examine plastering techniques, and strive to incorporate wattle and daub walls into modern structures.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: After a slow start, Japanese washing machines have become some of the most advanced in the world. We look at their development and the latest innovations.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
programme.desc: . Oils shape Japan's food culture, where households choose from a wide range of oils to suit each dish. This episode explores Japanese plant-based oils, focusing on flavour, safety, and health.

programme.title: Nhk Newsline
programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Backstage Japan
programme.desc: . Millions of people pass through Narita International Airport every year, unaware of the systems that ensure their safety and comfort. We'll dive into this hidden, complex world.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo

programme.title: NHK NEWS 7
programme.desc: NEWS7 is NHK's flagship domestic evening news program, bringing you the latest from our Tokyo studio. It's presented with English audio on NHK World for our international viewers.

programme.title: Today's Close-Up
programme.desc: Japanese authorities are trying to recover thousands of toy guns that can fire live rounds with potentially lethal effect. NHK investigated how these dangerous products entered circulation.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues. Yuko Fukushima, Aiko Doden, Takafumi Terui, Shoichiro Beppu, Hideki Nakayama, Takuma Yoshioka, Koichiro Tanaka, Chan-ju Kim, Abhishek Dhulia, Aki Shibuya.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 240. Matsuo Takahiro has gained international recognition for installations that fuse light with cutting-edge technology. He talks about how he creates his one-of-a-kind light art. Gene Otani.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: . In Uda, discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule, a sanctuary of the local culture and history.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . It's late at night, when all are fast asleep. A young man and woman huddle together along the veranda outside of a house, attending to a ... rooster. What could the pair be up to.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues. Yuko Fukushima, Aiko Doden, Takafumi Terui, Shoichiro Beppu, Hideki Nakayama, Takuma Yoshioka, Koichiro Tanaka, Chan-ju Kim, Abhishek Dhulia, Aki Shibuya.

programme.desc: Specialists rebuild a sturdy, mud-walled storehouse to safeguard cultural assets, scientifically examine plastering techniques, and strive to incorporate wattle and daub walls into modern structures.

programme.title: Japan Railway Journal
programme.desc: . See the company's plans to survive this time, following its previous triumphs over crises. Nathan Berry, Dennis Falt, Lyena Yonekura.
programme.category: 2015. Choshi Electric Railway in Chiba Prefecture posted its first loss in four years

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.desc: Specialists rebuild a sturdy, mud-walled storehouse to safeguard cultural assets, scientifically examine plastering techniques, and strive to incorporate wattle and daub walls into modern structures.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo
programme.desc: . NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues. Yuko Fukushima, Aiko Doden, Takafumi Terui, Shoichiro Beppu, Hideki Nakayama, Takuma Yoshioka, Koichiro Tanaka, Chan-ju Kim, Abhishek Dhulia, Aki Shibuya.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 240. Matsuo Takahiro has gained international recognition for installations that fuse light with cutting-edge technology. He talks about how he creates his one-of-a-kind light art. Gene Otani.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony
programme.desc: . In Uda, discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule, a sanctuary of the local culture and history.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . We visit one of the most famous cherry blossom spots in Edo. Flower viewing parties were for eating, drinking and making merry. It was also an opportunity for young men and women to mingle.

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.desc: . In winter, seasonal workers arrive at mandarin orange groves for the two-month harvest. Living in communal lodgings, they share their perspectives on the world and plan new paths for their lives. Soness Stevens, Hiroyuki Hamada, Koji Okubo, Yusuke Ito.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Spring
programme.desc: Hi, it's me, Tama! Today, my humans, Grandpa Makoto and Grandma Nobuko, left wearing strange outfits. That's because they're going to pick himetake bamboo shoots with two experts. I hope they get enough for me, too.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: After a slow start, Japanese washing machines have become some of the most advanced in the world. We look at their development and the latest innovations.

programme.title: Today's Close Up

programme.title: Asia Insight
programme.desc: . For the sake of their families, Nepalese men are working for the Russian military. Wages 10 times higher than in Nepal are promised, but as many go missing, their families await word on their fate. Vinay Murthy, Naoki Nomura, Haruo Sakitsu.

programme.title: The Imperial Palace
programme.desc: s and representing a fresh start for Japan, the new Imperial Palace showcases exceptional artistry and craftsmanship.

programme.title: Seasoning The Seasons
programme.desc: . This program visits places across Japan to introduce the charms of their local daily life and festivals fostered by the nation's long history. Tomohiro Nakano.

programme.title: Document 72 Hours
programme.desc: . A 24-hour bookstore in Tokyo attracts a diverse range of customers. For three days and nights, we asked people what they are buying - and why - in an age of digital books and online shopping. Masako Nishijima.

programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.desc: . Dig deeper into current affairs. Cutting-edge information and critical analysis on important issues confronting Japanese lives. Hiroko Kuniya, Takeshige Morimoto.

programme.title: Newsroom Tokyo

We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues. programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: , Episode 239. Tsuji Masachika is working on using sounds that birds dislike to keep them away from airplanes and prevent bird strikes. He talks about seeking ways for wild animals and humans to coexist in harmony. Gene Otani.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.desc: . Episode 3 of this series on COP30 in Brazil looks at AMOC, an ocean current in the Atlantic. It transports warm water to the north, but melting ice threatens to stop it entirely.

programme.title: Chatroom Japan
programme.desc: Kumamoto quakes.
programme.category: 2022. Do you know what to do when disaster strikes? If you don't
Andrew Mitchell from the UK has some stories to tell you from his own experience living through the

programme.title: Design X Stories
programme.desc: . Today's theme: Reimagining Analog Design. Analog is making a comeback, offering a human appeal to our five senses that digital lacks. Explore designs that reveal the charm and potential of analog.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.desc: . NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: FRONTRUNNERS
programme.desc: In Hiroshima, as atomic bomb survivors age, preserving their memories has become a pressing issue. We follow Sumioka Kenta's efforts to further the cause of peace, through the creativity of youth.

programme.title: Sumopedia
programme.desc: . Sumopedia enriches your sumo experience with insights into the sport’s techniques, traditions and legendary wrestlers. While the rules are simple, this iconic Japanese sport has far more depth than meets the eye.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.desc: . Episode 3 of this series on COP30 in Brazil looks at AMOC, an ocean current in the Atlantic. It transports warm water to the north, but melting ice threatens to stop it entirely.

programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-life
programme.desc: . A series of doodles by the famous artist Utagawa Kuniyoshi turn out to be carefully considered depictions of the kabuki actors of the day. What kind of artistic statement was he trying to make.
